title: mentha subacutidentata
intro: Mentha subacutidentata, also known as the Himalayan peppermint, is a species of mint native to the Himalayan region and parts of China. It is a perennial herb characterized by its square stem, green leaves, and small purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and combat fever. The leaves of Mentha subacutidentata can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are valued for their health benefits, including relaxation, improved digestion, and relief from respiratory issues.
